speeches.name: Jerome Powell
speeches.summary: Powell stated that the FOMC maintained the federal funds rate at 3.5-3.75%, described the policy stance as appropriate for dual mandate goals amid high uncertainty from Middle East developments, and affirmed data-dependent decisions with readiness to hike or cut if needed.

speeches.name: Lorie Logan
speeches.summary: Logan issued a statement dissenting from the FOMC decision following the April 28-29 meeting.

speeches.name: Christopher J. Waller
speeches.summary: Waller delivered a speech on modernizing Federal Reserve operations in the 21st century.

macro_fred.narrative: Leaning Neutral: Fed Funds at 3.64%, unemployment at 4.3%.
